items.title: Magpies struck by deadly mystery illness as carers report declining numbers

children.content: Researchers say they are still far from identifying the cause of a deadly paralytic illness spreading through populations of the iconic Australian birds.

items.title: WA cases surge in Australia's worst diphtheria outbreak 'for decades'

children.content: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

image.altText: A needle being filled with vaccine.

items.title: No long-term rentals but 200 short-stay listings in WA towns

children.content: There are renewed calls for a ban on short-stay rentals as a new report shows holiday homes vastly outnumber permanent rentals in small regional communities.

image.altText: A sign that says Nannup next to a road and greenery.

items.title: Court shutdown means justice takes much longer in Balgo

children.content: In this remote East Kimberley community, where the local court has been shut for almost two years due to security concerns, the closure of the nearest regional court from June 1 is raising concerns about justice.

image.altText: A map showing Balgo, Western Australia.

items.title: Stalking trial hears police officer felt 'smothered' by assistant commissioner

children.content: A WA police assistant commissioner faces trial accused of stalking a female subordinate officer and using a restricted computer system to track her movements.

image.altText: A woman with short hair and wearing a white top leaves the court after a trial.

items.title: Thirteen years after shark hooks, WA still grapples with deadly attacks

children.content: The approach to tackling shark attacks has changed dramatically since the 2013 catch-and-kill policy, with a focus on shark tagging and tracking. But it's never enough to stop fatal attacks.

articles.title: 'Devoted father and gentleman': Tributes flow for shark attack victim

children.content: The wife of 38-year-old Perth man Steven Mattaboni says her family's "hearts are irrevocably broken" after he was killed in a shark attack while spearfishing off the coast of Rottnest Island on Saturday.

image.altText: A man holding a little girl and a woman holding a baby with the beach in the background

articles.title: Friends witness 'horrific' shark attack that claimed life of spearfishing mate

children.content: A 38-year-old man had been spearfishing with friends when he was killed in a shark attack off Rottnest Island on Saturday morning.

image.altText: Police and ambulance parked near a jetty at a bay

items.title: An emergency permit of mouse bait has been approved by regulator

children.content: Farm groups have welcomed the chemical regulator's long-awaited decision to approve double-strength mouse baits amid plague numbers in Western Australia and South Australia.

items.title: Pheobe Bishop murder-accused's defence waiting for ballistics report

children.content: The defence team for Tanika Bromley, who is charged over the murder of Queensland teenager Pheobe Bishop, raises issues about delays in receiving parts of the brief of evidence.

items.title: 'Bro, there's a frog in my lettuce': Woolworths investigates salad bag surprise

items.title: Federal government intervenes again over China-linked stakes in WA rare earths miner

children.content: The federal treasurer orders another six companies to divest their holdings in Northern Minerals as concerns grow in Canberra that Chinese-backed investors are trying to seize control of the rare earths mining company.

items.title: 'Nowhere for me to exist': Growing up as a biracial child

children.content: KA was "stripped" of her culture as a child and has been on a journey to reclaim her connection to her Filipina heritage.

items.title: Six shops ordered to shut under new illegal tobacco laws
